channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Los clientes de SAP están tardando en implantar la IA de forma generalizada
item.description: Una nueva encuesta realizada por SAP a organizaciones clientes de las soluciones de la compañía muestra que, a pesar de experimentar con la IA, pocas han implementado esta tecnología (incuyendo la modalidad generativa) en sus empresas.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Cuatro pasos para optimizar el gasto empresarial en la nube
item.description: La proliferación de la computación en la nube ha transformado radicalmente la forma en que las empresas gestionan sus cargas de trabajo críticas, transformando la nube en una herramienta estratégica y un facilitador de negocios.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Russian hosting firm RUVDS takes server tech to the South Pole for high-speed data tests in extreme conditions
item.description: Exploring high-speed data at the world’s coldest frontier, RUVDS aims for Antarctic breakthrough after North Pole test.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Analítica prescriptiva: el siguiente nivel para impulsar el futuro de la experiencia del cliente
item.description: La analítica prescriptiva va un paso más allá y no solo predice lo que podría suceder, sino que recomienda las mejores acciones para lograr un resultado positivo.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: L’AI ha cambiato tutto: ecco perché per i CIO è la tecnologia del decennio
item.description: Nel 2024 le aziende italiane hanno continuato a investire sul digitale e il trend si confermerà nel 2025. Due le grandi priorità: cybersicurezza e intelligenza artificiale.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Toughbook 33mk4: Panasonic's New Rugged Tablet Delivers Enhanced Performance and Connectivity - Tech Review
item.description: Panasonic's TOUGHBOOK 33mk4, a new rugged tablet, offers advanced features like 5G connectivity, extended battery life, and MIL-STD 810H certification.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Navy looking at cloud as GFE to reduce CMMC burden
item.description: Jane Rathbun, the Department of the Navy’s CIO, said tools from NSA and NCIS along with a new initiative could help small firms meet CMMC requirements. The post Navy looking at cloud as GFE to reduce CMMC burden first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Bluesky's Explosive Growth: A Double-Edged Sword
item.description: Bluesky's user base has soared since the U.S. presidential election, but with growth comes growing pains, including bots and disinformation.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Tech expert warns of device that disables Wi-Fi cameras
item.description: It just takes a flick of a switch, and it can make your Wi-Fi camera stop working and miss the crucial video you need.
item.dc:creator: Elena Cousino, NewsNation

item.title: Windows 11 suffers more bugs in latest update, with the Start menu hit hard by some frustrating issues
item.description: Windows 11’s December update introduces visual glitches, problems that are torpedoing the Start menu, and a sprinkling of other bugs.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How (and why) to start using an RSS reader for news again
item.description: Imagine having a single app to keep up with your favorite news sources, YouTube channels, newsletters, and blogs, all in an easy-to-read format with no social media algorithms deciding what you see. That’s the idea behind RSS, a 25-year-old open standard for syndicating content from around the web.
item.dc:creator: Jared Newman, Fast Company

item.title: McDonalds delivery customers put at risk by possible data breach
item.description: Delivery system for McDonalds in India had a worrying bug, but luckily, threat actors didn't find it.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Many creatives are actually optimistic about the effects of AI
item.description: Creative workers are excited to be more… creative as AI and automation promises to cut down administrative work.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IT leaders’ top 9 takeaways from 2024
item.description: The past year was another one of rapid change, as economic cycles, business trends, and technology itself evolved at a breakneck pace.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Requisitos que deben cumplir los líderes de TI para navegar en la era de disrupción actual
item.description: Para su Informe sobre la preparación para la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa, la compañía de servicios de TI Avanade encuestó a más de 3.000 ejecutivos de TI y empresas de 10 países con, al menos, 500 millones de dólares en ingresos anuales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Overcoming modern observability challenges
item.description: Observability, at its core, is about collecting and analyzing data generated from applications, cloud computing resources, and edge devices to ensure software and services are run smoothly, user experiences are optimized, and resources are managed cost-effectively.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The exciting new world of Redis
item.description: If you’re a developer who wants the most feature-rich, high-performance version of Redis, your choice is clear: Redis and not a fork. If you have the time and inclination to dabble in ideological debates about open source licensing, well, you might make another choice.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Nissan and Honda to attempt a merger that would create the world's No. 3 automaker
item.description: Japanese automakers Nissan and Honda have announced plans to work toward a merger that would catapult them to a top position in an industry in the midst of tectonic shifts as it transitions away from its reliance on fossil fuels.
item.dc:creator: Mari Yamaguchi And Elaine Kurtenbach, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: La colaboración entre Seguridad y FinOps puede generar beneficios ocultos en la nube
item.description: Para las empresas que operan en la nube, la seguridad y la gestión de costes son preocupaciones crecientes.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 3D-printed or ‘ghost’ guns are a growing global security threat
item.description: Police investigating the shooting of UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson on Dec. 4, 2024, have announced that the suspected assailant had used a 3D-printed gun. Several high-profile crimes in recent years have involved this kind of homemade, or partially homemade, weapon.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: NASA's Parker Solar Probe aims to fly closer to the sun like never before
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A NASA spacecraft aims to fly closer to the sun than any object sent before. The Parker Solar Probe was launched in 2018 to get a close-up look at the sun. Since then, it has flown straight through the sun's corona: the outer atmosphere visible during a total solar eclipse.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: New leak says if your iPhone can run iOS 18, it should be able to run iOS 19 too
item.description: We have some hints about which iPhones and iPads are going to be able to run the software Apple unveils next year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: House Republicans brush aside Musk's primary threats
item.description: House Republicans are largely brushing aside Elon Musk’s threats to unseat members who supported the initial government funding measure he helped torpedo earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Everything new on Netflix in January 2025
item.description: We've made it to 2025, and a new year means brand new titles coming to Netflix. Here's the full list of movies and shows to add to your Netflix watchlist for January.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Amazon and Starbucks workers are on strike. Trump might have something to do with it
item.description: Amazon delivery drivers and Starbucks baristas are on strike in a handful of U.S. cities as they seek to exert pressure on the two major companies to recognize them as unionized employees or to meet demands for an inaugural labor contract.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Albania to close TikTok for a year blaming it for promoting violence among children
item.description: TIRANA, Albania (AP) — Albania's prime minister said Saturday the government will shut down the video service TikTok for one year, blaming it for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: LLAZAR SEMINI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Democrat accuses Musk of tanking spending bill over China restrictions
item.description: The top Democrat on the House Appropriations Committee accused Elon Musk of tanking a bipartisan spending bill because it included a provision that could limit his businesses' ability to operate in China. In a Friday letter to congressional leaders, Rep. Rosa DeLauro (D-Conn.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: Musk says he'll fund 'moderate' Democratic primary challengers
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk on Thursday claimed he will fund moderate primary challenges to incumbent Democrats in heavy-blue districts around the country.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: VA EHR to resume rollout in mid-2026, after clinicians see fewer crashes
item.description: The VA says clinicians are seeing fewer interruptions from the new EHR, and that the average user now sees “near zero interruptions,” such as freezes or lags. The post VA EHR to resume rollout in mid-2026, after clinicians see fewer crashes first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Scholz dismisses Musk's assertion that only a far-right party can 'save' Germany
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz on Friday dismissed an assertion by Elon Musk that only a far-right party can “save Germany,” but said that freedom of opinion "also goes for multibillionaires.” Germany is expected to vote in an early election on Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Kazakhstan’s Digital Policeman: Pioneering the future of law enforcement
item.description: In the era of digital transformation, public safety stands at a critical crossroads. Law enforcement agencies globally are under increasing scrutiny to enhance transparency, efficiency, and trust within their communities.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Amazon workers are striking at multiple facilities. Here's what you should know
item.description: Amazon workers affiliated with the Teamsters union are on strike for a second day at seven of the company’s delivery hubs just days before Christmas.
item.dc:creator: Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Elon Musk considers funding Nigel Farage's populist party, a move that could shake up UK politics
item.description: LONDON (AP) — It’s a photo that sent a tremor through British politics: Elon Musk flanked by British politician Nigel Farage and a wealthy backer, in front of a gilt-framed painting of a young Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Massive data breach at federal credit union exposes 240,000 members
item.description: A credit union with over 240,000 members recently revealed it was targeted by cybercriminals, resulting in a data breach that was part of a two-month attack by hackers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Apple complains Meta requests risk privacy in spat over EU efforts to widen access to iPhone tech
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple complained that requests from Meta Platforms for access to its operating software threaten user privacy, in a spat fueled by the European Union's intensifying efforts to get the iPhone maker to open up to products from tech rivals.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Create searchable Bluesky bookmarks with R
item.description: The Bluesky social network is seeping into the mainstream, thanks in part to people unhappy with changes at X (formerly Twitter). “It’s not some untouched paradise, but it’s got a spark of something refreshing,” Jan Beger, global head of AI Advocacy at GE HealthCare, posted on LinkedIn last month.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Go 1.24 brings full support for generic type aliases
item.description: Go 1.24, a planned update to Google‘s popular open-source programming language, has reached the release candidate stage.
item.dc:creator: Info World
